title: Anti-Hydration Rules
description: **SSR HTML MUST BE IDENTICAL to first WASM render**

title: SSR Effects and Browser API Safety
description: **BROWSER APIs MUST NEVER RUN ON SERVER**

title: Visual State Declaration
description: **UI components expose semantic state via data-attributes. CSS + tokens decide visual representation.**

title: Theme and Token Governance
description: **Tokens are not owned by Tailwind. Theme is not a UI concern. Apps never import design system internals.**

title: Overlay Islands (Client-Only Architecture)
description: **Overlays dinâmicos** (com listas reativas) + **SSR** = **Hydration Hell**

description: `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` fails in Leptos callbacks because:

title: Modal Reactive State Management
description: Passing `String` props to modals = **values never update**:

title: Multi-Callback Ownership
description: **Multiple callbacks = moved value errors:**

description: **Enunciado curto (para lembrar fácil):**

description: **Enunciado curto (para lembrar fácil):**

description: **Enunciado curto (para lembrar fácil):**

description: **Enunciado curto (para lembrar fácil):**

description: **Enunciado curto (para lembrar fácil):**

title: Client vs Server
description: **Enunciado curto (para lembrar fácil):**

title: canon-rule-19-streaming-vs-snapshot

title: Canonical Color Tokens vs Semantic Intents
description: Components consume a **stable contract of theme tokens**. Semantic intents (success, warning, info) are **application-layer mappings**, not theme-layer requirements.

title: Tailwind v4 + Rust Integration
description: Tailwind v4's JIT compiler **cannot reliably parse Rust syntax** for arbitrary value generation. All Tailwind utilities used in Rust components must be **pre-compiled** via explicit class definitions

title: State Tokens (Hover, Focus, Disabled, Pressed)
description: Interactive states must use **consistent, theme-independent opacity values** rather than color variants. This ensures state feedback works across all themes without requiring theme authors to define s

title: Density & Size Scaling
description: UI density and component sizes must be **data-attribute driven** and **mathematically consistent** across the design system. Size scales follow a **modular scale** (1.25x ratio) for visual harmony.

title: Theme Presets Contract
description: Theme presets define **color palettes only**. All other design decisions (spacing, typography, radius, shadows) are **system-wide constants** that themes cannot override. This ensures visual consisten

title: Elevation & Shadow System
description: Elevation creates visual hierarchy through **consistent shadow layers**. Shadows are **system-wide constants** that themes cannot override, ensuring predictable z-axis relationships across all themes.

title: Motion & Timing Tokens
description: Motion creates **purposeful transitions** that guide user attention and provide feedback. All animations use **system-wide timing tokens** that respect user preferences and maintain consistency across

title: Responsive Grid Contract
description: Responsive layout uses **mobile-first breakpoints** and a **12-column grid system** with consistent gutters. Breakpoints and grid settings are **system-wide constants** that ensure predictable layout

title: Typography Contract
description: Typography creates **information hierarchy** and **readability**. Font stacks, sizes, weights, and line heights are **system-wide constants** that ensure visual consistency and performance across all

title: Iconography System
description: Icons are **SVG-based, inline, and accessible**. The system uses a **single canonical icon library** (Lucide) with consistent sizing, coloring, and ARIA patterns. Icon fonts are prohibited.

title: Leptos Resource Consumption Contract
description: `Resource` in Leptos is a **reactive signal**, not a `Future`. It must be consumed via reactive tracking (`.get()`, `.read()`, `.with()`), never via `.await`. This rule prevents silent render failures

title: UI Data Surfaces
description: Data source strategies (streaming, pagination, infinite scroll) are **adapters**, not components. A single primitive (`VirtualTable`) handles all rendering, while adapters provide data.

title: Domain Components and Commands
description: Domain components **NEVER** execute commands directly. They only **OBSERVE** state. Commands are **ALWAYS** emitted by an explicit "Command Component" layer. This rule enforces CQRS at the UI level.

title: Orchestrators
description: Orchestrators are the **GLUE** between Domain Components (READ) and Command Components (WRITE). They coordinate the CQRS cycle at the UI level without implementing business logic or rendering complexi

title: Demo Components & Ephemeral State
description: Demo components optimize for **clarity**, not realism. Ephemeral state with explicit signals is preferred over production patterns when the goal is documentation, learning, or visual showcase.

title: Command Palette & Intent Surfaces
description: Command Palette is not a "nice-to-have widget" — it's the **command bus of the UI**. It decouples user intent from visual affordances, enabling discovery, accessibility, automation, and scaling withou

title: Tree, Context & Selection
description: Tree components expose **structure and selection**, never actions. Context flows from Tree → SelectionContext → Command Registry, enabling contextual commands without coupling tree navigation to domai

title: Context Provider Pattern
description: Use Context Providers to eliminate prop drilling and enable global access to selection state. Components consume context via hooks instead of receiving props.

title: Drag & Drop as Intent (Not Action)
description: **Drag & Drop is an input gesture, not business logic.**

title: Provider Singleton & Runtime Separation
description: **Interaction Scope:** A subtree responsible for a continuous unit of interaction (drag operation, editor session, workflow step). Each scope maintains its own reactive runtime.

title: Callbacks as Commands
description: **UI callbacks MUST emit commands, not execute mutations directly.**

title: Command History as First-Class Runtime
description: **Command History MUST be a first-class provider in the application runtime.**

title: Client-Only Runtime Islands
description: **Runtime code that depends on browser APIs MUST be guarded with `#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]`.**

title: canon-rule-54-render-must-be-total
description: **Rendering must always be total and non-panicking.

title: Canonical CSS Entry Points
description: Design System CSS **MUST** be consumed exclusively through **canonical workspace packages**.

title: Monorepo CSS Build Pipeline
description: CSS in a monorepo **MUST** be produced as **build artifacts**, then **copied** into canonical workspace packages.

title: PostCSS Canon Configuration
description: PostCSS in Canon projects **MUST**:

title: Leptos Assets Dev Constraint
description: Leptos dev server **ONLY serves first-level files** from `assets-dir`.

title: CSS Cascade Ownership
description: There must be **exactly ONE owner** of the final CSS cascade.

title: CSS Artifacts Are Immutable
description: Generated CSS artifacts are **immutable outputs**.

title: No Relative CSS Imports
description: Relative imports in CSS **DO NOT SCALE** and are forbidden outside the local package.

title: Single Source of Truth for Design Tokens
description: **Design tokens MUST exist in exactly ONE canonical location.**

title: Leptos Reactivity - No .get() Outside Closures
description: **Reactive values MUST be accessed inside closures when used in reactive contexts.**

title: CSS Build Pipeline is Mandatory
description: **CSS is a build-time artifact, not a runtime feature.**

title: data-theme Sync is ThemeProvider Responsibility
description: **The ThemeProvider MUST synchronize theme state to the DOM.**

title: Workbench Setup Checklist
description: **Every new workbench/app MUST pass a health check before development begins.**

title: Leptos CSR Does NOT Load CSS via `<Stylesheet />`
description: In Client-Side Rendering (CSR) mode, `leptos_meta::<Stylesheet />` is **NOT** the source of truth for global CSS.

title: Asset Must Exist in Final dist/
description: Every asset referenced by the application **MUST** physically exist in the final `dist/` directory that is served to users.

title: Trunk Only Serves What's in dist/
description: Trunk serves **ONLY** the content that exists in the final `dist/` directory.

title: CSS Pipeline Requires Health Checks
description: Every example, application, and component library **MUST** have automated validation that CSS is correctly built and contains expected content.

title: Debug Theme by Verifying File First
description: Before debugging tokens, signals, Effects, or DOM manipulation in theme-related issues, **ALWAYS** verify that the CSS file exists and was loaded correctly.

title: Layout H1 Prohibition
description: **Layouts MUST NOT contain `<h1>` elements. The `<h1>` tag is reserved exclusively for Page components.**

title: ComponentPage Template Contract
description: **Component documentation pages MUST use the `ComponentPage` template from the design system. Product code MUST only pass data as props, never reimplementing page structure.**

title: Block Semantic HTML
description: **Blocks MUST use semantic HTML with proper ARIA attributes, heading hierarchy, and accessible table markup. All documentation blocks MUST follow WCAG 2.1 guidelines.**

title: Primitive CSS Prohibition
description: Primitives provide **semantic HTML structure only**. They MUST NOT contain:

title: Navigation vs Action Component Contract
description: **Navigation uses `<a>`.**

title: Monorepo Path Stability
description: Configuration files MUST NOT hardcode internal monorepo paths.

title: Token Definition Completeness
description: Every UI component MUST use **100% of applicable canonical and contextual tokens**.

title: ComponentPage Template Contract
description: `ComponentPage` is a **template contract** that provides canonical layout structure for component documentation.

title: Workspace Watch Configuration
description: `cargo-leptos watch` does NOT automatically monitor path dependencies outside the current workspace.

title: Flex Layout Ownership
description: In flex container hierarchies, **child growth is determined by the parent**, not the child itself.

title: CSS Build Pipeline Health
description: CSS changes MUST be **explicitly compiled** before they take effect in the application.

title: Layout Zones Contract
description: **Every element knows where it belongs.**

title: SVG Dark Mode Contract
description: SVG assets referenced via `` tags **CANNOT** inherit `currentColor` or CSS properties.

title: Leptos Asset Pipeline in Dev Mode
description: `cargo leptos watch` (via `make dev`) uses **Leptos asset serving**, NOT Trunk.

title: Children vs ChildrenFn Contract
description: `Children` and `ChildrenFn` are **NOT interchangeable**.

title: Leptos SSR Script Placement
description: **Imperative JavaScript MUST be injected in `shell()`, NOT in `index.html`.**

title: Primitives Must Never Touch Browser APIs
description: **UI Primitives are structural only. They MUST NOT access browser APIs.**

title: Hydration Is DOM Replacement, Not Enhancement
description: **Hydration replaces the DOM. It does not enhance it.**

title: Markdown and Code Blocks Are Render-Only
description: **Markdown components MUST only render structure, never behavior.**

title: If It Works in Prod but Not Dev, Suspect Hydration Order
description: **Behavior differences between dev and prod indicate hydration or script timing issues.**

title: Leptos WASM Dev Builds Must Use Release Mode
description: **Interactive Leptos applications that compile to WASM MUST run dev/watch in `--release` mode.**

title: Leptos Workspace Features Must Be Explicit
description: **Leptos features (especially `nightly`) MUST be declared explicitly in `[workspace.dependencies]`, not delegated to member crates.**

title: SSR Requires Complete HTML Shell
description: **Leptos SSR applications MUST provide a complete HTML document shell with explicit `<html>`, `<head>`, and `<body>` tags.**

title: SSR Requires Explicit Provider Tree
description: **Leptos SSR applications MUST explicitly wrap the component tree with all required Providers in the App component.**

title: Leptos 0.8 Requires Floating Nightly Toolchain
description: **Leptos 0.8 applications MUST use unpinned `nightly` toolchain and unpinned minor versions in Cargo.toml.**

title: Axum + Leptos SSR Closures Must Own State
description: **When configuring Axum Router with Leptos SSR, each closure MUST own its copy of `LeptosOptions` via explicit `clone()` before the `move` keyword.**

title: CSR Islands Must Not Own Routing or Providers
description: **WASM islands in an SSR-first application MUST NOT create their own Router or provide global Providers.**

title: Build Orchestrators Must Be Workspace-Scoped
description: **Build orchestration configuration (cargo-leptos, trunk, etc.) MUST be declared in the workspace root `Cargo.toml`, not in individual member crates.**

title: Workbench Assets Must Be Product-Scoped
description: **Illustrative assets belong to the product, never to the design system.**

title: Runtime JS Is Shell Infrastructure
description: **Components declare intent. The shell executes effects.**

title: Critical Runtime JS Must Be Inline in SSR
description: **Critical runtime JS must execute before hydration.**

title: AutoReload Breaks Script Order Guarantees
description: **AutoReload may reorder or inject scripts unpredictably.**

title: canon-rule-105-visual-indicators-must-have-a-single-owner
description: **A visual indicator MUST have exactly one canonical owner in the component hierarchy.**

title: canon-rule-106-ui-neutralizes-hostile-css-not-primitives
description: **CSS neutralization is a UI concern, never a primitive concern.**

title: Token Architecture & Theme Specificity
description: **:root defines structure. Themes define colors.**

title: Visual Surfaces Contract
description: **Components don't choose appearance. They obey surfaces.**

title: Model First, CSS Second
description: **CSS never fixes an architecturally invalid model.**

title: UI Owns Visual Style
description: **Only the UI layer can apply visual style.**

title: States Are Data, Not Style
description: **State is data, not appearance.**

title: Single Visual Authority
description: **A component has a single visual authority.**

title: Reset Awareness & CSS Boundaries
description: **UI components must assume the existence of global resets.**

title: WASM Externref Table Limits
description: **Components must not create unbounded JS↔WASM references.**

title: Design System Callbacks Are Props, Not Handlers
description: **UI components receive callbacks as props. They DO NOT create handlers inline.**

title: View<\_> Type Boundary Prohibition
description: **`View<_>` IS NOT A BOUNDARY TYPE**

title: No #[prop(optional, into)] in UI Layer
description: **UI layer components MUST NOT use `#[prop(optional, into)]` — use explicit `Option<T>` types instead.**

title: DOM Events vs Semantic Callbacks Boundary
description: **Primitives use `on:event` (DOM events). UI components use `on_event` (semantic callbacks). Never wrap callbacks in `Some()` at UI layer.**

title: StoredValue for Non-Copy Values in view! Closures
description: **Non-Copy values captured by closures inside `view!` MUST be wrapped in `StoredValue` — never moved directly.**

title: No Conditional Rendering with .then() Closures
description: **Conditional rendering MUST use `<Show>` component — never `condition.then(|| view!)`.**

title: Component Architecture Taxonomy and Contracts
description: **Every component MUST be classified into exactly one architectural type, and MUST respect the contracts of its layer.**

title: Primitive Contract Types
description: **Primitives render semantic HTML with zero logic, zero state, zero styling. Every Primitive MUST be classified as Pure, Interactive, or Container.**

title: UI Component Contracts
description: **UI components bridge Primitives and Application code by normalizing props, managing visual state, and emitting semantic callbacks. Every UI component MUST be classified as Adapter, Controlled, or Co

title: Component Domain Contracts
description: **Components own domain state, implement business logic, and orchestrate workflows. Every Component MUST be classified as Stateful or Orchestrator.**

title: Block Composition Contracts
description: **Blocks compose Components into semantic page sections without domain state or routing. Every Block MUST be classified as Semantic or Interactive.**

title: Layout Shell and Zone Contracts
description: **Layouts define structural boundaries and positioning without business logic. Every Layout MUST be classified as Shell or Zone.**

title: SSR Event Safety
description: **Blocks MUST be treated as SSR-static structures. Interactivity in blocks MUST be delegated or CSR-only.**

title: Controlled UI Contract
description: **UI components that control reactive state MUST accept `Signal<T>` or `RwSignal<T>`, never plain `T` for stateful props.**

title: Reactive Boundary Ownership
description: **Non-`Copy` values captured by reactive boundaries (`<Show>`, `<For>`, `<Suspense>`, `<Transition>`) MUST either:**

title: Layout Composition Over Abstraction
description: **Layouts MUST be structural wrappers and MUST NOT orchestrate composition logic or slot abstraction.**

title: Children Consumption Locality
description: **`Children` MUST be consumed in the closest possible component to their final render location.**

title: Layouts Are CSS and Semantics Only
description: **Layouts MUST provide only semantic containers and CSS contracts, never behavioral or reactive logic.**

title: UI vs Layout Responsibility Boundary
description: **Layouts define spatial zones; UI components define interaction and behavior.**

title: Controllers Are CSR-Only
description: **Controllers MUST exist and execute exclusively in CSR (wasm32), never during SSR.**

title: Providers Must Have a Single Owner
description: **Every Provider MUST have exactly one owning component responsible for its lifecycle.**

title: Children Must Be Consumed Immediately
description: **`Children` MUST be consumed immediately in the component that receives them and MUST NOT be forwarded across abstraction layers.**

title: Slots Are UI-Level Only
description: **Component slots MUST be defined and consumed exclusively at the UI component level, never in Layouts or Shells.**

title: Layouts Are Structural, Not Behavioral
description: **Layouts MUST define structure and zones only and MUST NOT contain behavior, state, or logic.**

title: CSR Composition Belongs to the App Layer
description: **All CSR-only composition, event wiring, and behavioral orchestration MUST occur in the application layer, never in layouts or design-system components.**

title: canon-rule-142-children-must-always-be-optional
description: Public UI components frequently evolve. Requiring `children` as a mandatory prop creates brittle APIs, breaks composability, and forces artificial wrappers.

title: canon-rule-143-ui-must-be-hydration-deterministic
description: SSR + Hydration requires **bit-for-bit identical HTML** between server and client at hydration time.

title: canon-rule-144-providers-expose-state-apps-own-interaction
description: Providers are architectural primitives responsible for **state exposure**, not user interaction.

title: canon-rule-145-ui-must-not-mutate-global-state-implicitly
description: Implicit global state mutation hides side effects and breaks predictability.

title: canon-rule-146-ui-content-must-be-ssr-stable
description: Leptos SSR performs DOM walking during hydration.

title: canon-rule-147-reactive-closures-are-data-not-structure
description: Reactive closures in Leptos are evaluated at different times in SSR and CSR.

title: canon-rule-148-ui-must-never-infer-intent-from-state
description: State represents **what is**, not **why it changed**.

title: canon-rule-149-controllers-must-be-temporal-not-structural
description: Controllers exist to manage **time**, **async**, and **coordination**.

title: canon-rule-150-ui-must-be-deterministic-under-ssr-and-hydration
description: SSR and hydration require **byte-for-byte compatible HTML**.

title: canon-rule-151-visual-feedback-must-never-encode-application-state
description: Visual indicators exist to reflect state, not define it.

title: canon-rule-152-provider-callback-hydration-ownership
description: In SSR + Hydration environments (Leptos/Tachys), callbacks attached to DOM events

title: canon-rule-153-layouts-must-be-event-free-during-hydration
description: During SSR + Hydration, the DOM generated on the server **must be structurally and behaviorally identical** to the DOM expected by the client at hydration time.

title: Deterministic Layout via Canonical CSS (SSR-Safe)
description: **All layout positioning MUST be resolved exclusively by canonical CSS, never by runtime DOM manipulation or utility-based composition.**

title: CSS Token Contract Is Architecture
description: **CSS design tokens MUST use a single, deterministic format (numeric HSL) and NEVER mix formats within a design system.**

title: CSS Variable Scope Is Non-Negotiable
description: **CSS variable mappings MUST be defined in the same selector scope as the source variables they reference.**

title: Design System CSS Must Be Build-Time Flattened
description: **Applications MUST consume design system CSS as a single, flattened file with zero runtime `@import` statements.**

title: Design System Packages Are Immutable Contracts (No Direct File Imports)
description: **Applications MUST consume design system artifacts only through versioned package exports, never through direct file system imports.**

title: UI CSS Must Be Fail-Safe, Token-Only, and Attribute-Scoped
description: **Every UI CSS file MUST be fail-safe by construction: token-only, data-attribute–scoped, and resilient to partial or missing UI/Primitive wiring.**

title: First App Must Not Require CSS Build
description: **The first CanonRS application must run with prebuilt CSS and no CSS build step.**

title: Canonical CSS Load Order Is Mandatory
description: **CanonRS CSS must always load in a strict, predefined order.**

title: Providers Are Infrastructure, Not UI
description: **Providers must never be implemented or exposed as UI components.**

title: DOM Effects Are Hydration-Only
description: **DOM mutations must only run under the `hydrate` feature.**

title: WASM and JS Assets Must Be Served by SSR
description: **All `/pkg` assets must be served explicitly by the SSR server.**

title: CanonRS Workbench Is a Canonical Reference
description: **A working CanonRS Workbench implementation defines canonical behavior.**

title: Dist Is Read-Only
description: **The dist/ directory is a build artifact and must never be edited manually.**

title: CanonRS CSS Has a Single Entrypoint
description: **CanonRS must expose exactly one canonical CSS entrypoint.**

title: UI Must Declare Required Tokens
description: **Every UI component must rely only on tokens that are explicitly defined.**

title: Token Import Order Is Architectural
description: **Token import order defines system correctness.**

title: HTML and CSS Must Share the Same Contract
description: **Rendered HTML attributes must exactly match CSS selectors.**

title: Phantom Variables Are Forbidden
description: **Every referenced variable must be defined and traceable.**

title: The Site Does Not Own Design
description: **Applications consume design; they do not define it.**

title: CSS Is a First-Class System
description: **CSS in CanonRS is a governed system, not decoration.**

title: canon-rule-174-tokens-are-compile-time-contracts
description: _This rule makes token chaos architecturally impossible._

title: Dark/Light Is a CSS Concern, Not a Component Concern
description: **Components must never reference `.dark` class or implement dark/light logic—theme system handles all context resolution.**

title: Governance Is the Single Source of Truth
description: **All authoritative decisions in CanonRS flow through governance.**

title: canonrs.css Is a Generated Artifact, Never a Design Surface
description: **The `canonrs.css` file is generated output—manual edits are forbidden and will be overwritten on next build.**

title: Contracts Are Read-Only APIs (DEPRECATED)
description: This rule is **fully superseded** by:

title: No Visual Surface Tokens in Core or Families
description: **Core and Family tokens MUST NOT define visual surface values; all visual surfaces MUST be resolved at the theme layer.**

title: Semantic Token Names Must Follow Canonical Suffixes
description: **Semantic tokens must use standardized suffixes exclusively: `-bg`, `-fg`, `-border`—never custom variants like `-surface`, `-color`, or `-text`.**

title: Token Cascade Is Architecture, Not Convention
description: **The token cascade (Preset → Theme → Semantic → Family → Component) is an architectural constraint enforced by the framework—not a coding convention.**

title: Semantic Role Must Affect Visual Contrast
description: **Components with different semantic roles must have visually distinct backgrounds—feedback states cannot share the same background as neutral surfaces.**

title: Overlay Positioning Is a Primitive, Not a Controller Concern
description: **All overlay positioning logic MUST live in a dedicated primitive, never in controllers or CSS alone.**

title: Floating Overlays Expose Position Only via CSS Variables
description: **Floating overlay primitives MUST expose computed positioning exclusively via CSS custom properties as a public contract.**

title: Overlays Must Be Anchor-Relative
description: **Every overlay MUST be positioned relative to an explicit anchor element.**

title: Overlay Visibility Is Controlled Only via data-state
description: **Overlay visibility MUST be controlled exclusively via the `data-state` attribute.**

title: Floating Primitives Enforce CSS-Variable-Only Positioning
description: **Floating primitives MUST enforce that positioning is communicated only via CSS custom properties.**

title: Overlay Positioning Is Not UI Logic
description: **Controllers and UI components MUST NOT compute or decide overlay positioning.**

title: Pages Must Delegate Wiring to Page Behaviors Only
description: **Pages MUST delegate all client-side wiring to a corresponding page-level behavior and MUST NOT contain logic themselves.**

title: Page Behaviors Must Not Implement New Logic
description: **Page behaviors MUST NOT implement business logic, calculations, or state machines.**

title: Framework Behaviors Are the Only Source of Interactivity
description: **All interactive behavior MUST be implemented exclusively inside framework behaviors.**

title: UI Components Are Pure Render Functions
description: **UI components MUST be pure render functions with no side effects or behavior.**

title: Interactive Components Require Explicit ID
description: **Interactive components that attach client-side behaviors MUST require an explicit `id` prop without default value or automatic generation.**

title: SSR/CSR Separation for WASM Bloat Prevention
description: **Design system code MUST be separated by compilation target: SSR-only code compiles only for server, CSR-only code compiles only for WASM client.**

title: Feature Flags Are Architectural Boundaries
description: **Feature flags define hard architectural boundaries, not conditional shortcuts.**

title: Runtime Crates Must Not Leak Cross-Target Types
description: **Runtime crates must never exchange concrete runtime types across targets.**

title: Server Adapters Are Integration Code
description: **Server adapters are integration glue, not framework architecture.**

title: Shared Crates Must Be Zero-Dependency Runtime
description: **Shared crates contain only pure, runtime-agnostic contracts.**

title: Presets Are the Only Source of Color Values
description: **Absolute color values (HSL, hex, RGB) must only exist in preset definitions.**

title: Themes Resolve Context, Not Palette
description: **Theme files (light/dark) map semantic meaning to preset variables—never define color values.**

title: Semantic Tokens Are the Only Bridge Between Theme and Families
description: **Family tokens must reference semantic tokens exclusively—never preset or theme tokens directly.**

title: Theme Files Must Be Last in the Cascade
description: **Theme files (light/dark overrides) must be imported after all other token and component CSS.**

title: UI Tokens Must Bind to Semantic Tokens, Never to Presets
description: **Family tokens (UI contracts like `--button-primary-bg`) must reference semantic tokens (`--semantic-*`), never preset tokens (`--color-*`).**

title: UI Inventory Is Fixed and Canonical
description: **The UI inventory is a fixed, canonical contract.**

title: Preset Switching Must Never Change Component CSS
description: **Changing theme preset (amber → blue → green) must only change CSS variable values—never component structure or selectors.**

title: A Working Theme Toggle Is Proof of Correct Token Architecture
description: **If theme toggle breaks visual rendering, the error is in token architecture—not JavaScript or component logic.**

title: Axum Version Must Match Adapter Version
description: **An application MUST use the exact Axum version required by its server adapter.**

title: cdylib Is Mandatory for Hydration
description: **Any Leptos application that supports hydration MUST declare `cdylib` as a crate type.**

title: SSR Meta Requires Explicit HTML Shell
description: **When using `leptos_meta` in SSR, an explicit HTML shell MUST be provided.**

title: Hydration Bootstrap Is Tooling-Owned
description: **The WASM hydration bootstrap MUST be owned by the build tooling, not the application.**

title: Streaming SSR Requires Explicit Executor Initialization
description: **Streaming SSR MUST explicitly initialize an async executor before rendering.**

title: lib.rs Owns Application Structure and UI Semantics
description: **`lib.rs` is the sole owner of application structure, UI composition, routing, and semantic intent.**

title: main.rs Owns Runtime and Server Bootstrap Exclusively
description: **`main.rs` MUST contain only runtime initialization and server bootstrap logic.**

title: App Components Must Not Define HTML Structure
description: **Application components MUST NOT define `<html>`, `<head>`, or `<body>` structure.**

title: Entry Points Must Be Explicit, Public, and Isolated
description: **All SSR and CSR entrypoints MUST be explicit, public, and feature-isolated.**

title: Legacy Rendering APIs Are Forbidden
description: **Deprecated or legacy rendering APIs MUST NOT be used in new or migrated systems.**

title: Leptos Product Must Declare Consistent Ports Across Workspace and Local Config
description: **`site-addr` and `reload-port` MUST match exactly between `[[workspace.metadata.leptos]]` and product `Leptos.toml`.**

title: Workspace Metadata Must Define Unique Build Targets Per Product
description: **Each `[[workspace.metadata.leptos]]` block MUST specify a unique `site-root` to prevent output collision.**

title: Leptos.toml Critical Fields Must Align With Workspace Metadata
description: **`site-addr`, `reload-port`, `bin-features`, and `lib-features` MUST match exactly between workspace metadata and product Leptos.toml. Path fields (`style-file`, `assets-dir`) MAY differ due to conte

title: Workspace Members Must Be Fully Resolvable by Cargo Metadata
description: **All paths in `[workspace.members]` MUST resolve to valid `Cargo.toml` files and appear in `cargo metadata --format-version 1` output.**

title: Feature Flag Scopes Must Not Leak Between Products
description: **Each `[[workspace.metadata.leptos]]` block MUST define `bin-features` and `lib-features` that apply ONLY to its specified `bin-package` and `lib-package`, with no cross-contamination.**

title: Workspace Package Names Must Use Hyphens Consistently
description: **All package names, bin names, and leptos config names MUST use hyphens (`-`), never underscores (`_`), with the understanding that Rust internally converts hyphens to underscores for module names.**

title: Workspace Dependency Version Policy Must Prevent ABI Drift Without Blocking Patch Compatibility
description: **Workspace-level runtime dependencies MUST prevent multiple-version ABI conflicts while preserving controlled patch compatibility.**

title: Workspace Leptos Paths Must Be Root-Relative
description: **All path fields in `[[workspace.metadata.leptos]]` (`style-file`, `assets-dir`) MUST be relative to the workspace root, not to the product directory.**

title: Products Must Inherit Workspace Dependencies
description: **Product crates MUST use `{ workspace = true }` for all dependencies declared in `[workspace.dependencies]`, never redeclaring versions.**

title: Workspace Must Define WASM Target Configuration
description: **Workspace MUST have `.cargo/config.toml` configuring `wasm32-unknown-unknown` target with optimization flags.**

title: Design System CSS Must Be Consumed as an Independent Artifact
description: **The CanonRS design system CSS MUST be consumed by products as an independent, prebuilt artifact, never as an input to utility or product-specific build pipelines.**

title: WASM Artifact Budget Is a Hard Contract
description: **Every WASM artifact MUST declare and enforce a maximum size budget at build time, failing compilation if exceeded.**

title: SSR Crates Must Be Link-Time Isolated From WASM Graph
description: **No server-side crate or server-only dependency may appear in the WASM target dependency graph — even transitively.**

title: Product Builds Must Be Deterministic From Workspace Root
description: **All product builds MUST be executed from workspace root with zero dependence on current working directory.**

title: CSS Cascade Order Is Build-Enforced Architecture
description: The CanonRS CSS cascade order is an architectural invariant enforced by the bundler.

title: Tokens Engine Is the Single Source of CSS Truth
description: All design tokens MUST originate exclusively from the CanonRS Tokens Engine.

title: UI Layer Must Consume Semantic Tokens, Never Theme Directly
description: UI components MUST consume only semantic tokens (`--color-*`).

title: CanonRS CLI Defines Workspace Topology
description: The CanonRS CLI is the single authority responsible for defining, generating, and reorganizing workspace topology.

title: CLI Owns Leptos Metadata Blocks
description: All `[[workspace.metadata.leptos]]` blocks are generated and synchronized exclusively by the CanonRS CLI.

title: Workspace Dependency Graph Must Be Acyclic
description: The workspace crate dependency graph must be a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG).

title: CLI Sync Operations Must Be Idempotent
description: Running `canonrs sync` multiple times without structural changes must produce zero diffs.

title: Generated Artifacts Must Not Be Committed
description: Generated artifacts must never be committed to the repository.

title: Tokens Engine Is Build Infrastructure, Not Runtime Code
description: The tokens-engine is a build-time tool.

title: Cascade Order Is a Contract, Not a Preference
description: The CSS cascade order is part of the architectural contract.

title: data-theme Is the Only Theme Activation Boundary
description: [data-theme] is the only valid activation boundary for themes.

title: Semantic Layer Is a Mandatory Abstraction
description: UI, Blocks and Root must consume semantic tokens only.

title: Families Must Not Leak Global State
description: Family tokens exist for component-domain grouping.

title: CSS Bundle Must Be Layer-Free
description: The final distributed artifact (`canonrs.bundle.css`) MUST NOT contain:

title: CSS Entry Order Is Architectural, Not Cosmetic
description: The entry generation order in canonrs.css is a hard architectural contract.

title: Tokens Engine Is the Only Source of Truth
description: No CSS token file inside .generated/ may be edited manually.

title: Products Must Not Depend on .generated CSS
description: Products MUST consume only the distributed artifact:

title: Design System Must Not Depend on Products
description: Crates classified as `design-system`, `ui`, `tokens-engine`, or `shared`

title: All Build Artifacts Must Be Statically Identifiable
description: All generated artifacts (CSS, WASM, bundles, manifests)

title: Theme Switching Must Be Attribute-Driven
description: Theme switching MUST occur exclusively via DOM attribute mutation:

title: Token Cascade Order Is Immutable
description: Token generation order is fixed:

title: CanonRS Must Build Independently of Products
description: CanonRS (tokens-engine + UI + CSS) MUST compile and generate artifacts without any product crate present.

title: CLI Is the Sole Authority Over Workspace Generation
description: **The CanonRS CLI is the only authority allowed to generate, update, or restructure `.canonrs/workspace`.**

title: Generated Workspace Is Ephemeral and Immutable
description: **`.canonrs/workspace` is an ephemeral artifact and must never be treated as source code.**

title: Tokens Engine Is a Mandatory Pre-Build Step
description: **The tokens-engine must execute before any CanonRS application build or development server starts.**

title: Mode Drives Build Profiles
description: **Application mode (ssr | csr | hybrid) defined in `canonrs.toml` is the single source of truth for build profile selection.**

title: Products Must Not Define Build Topology
description: **Products define business logic only. Build topology is governed exclusively by CanonRS CLI.**

title: CLI Autodiscovery Must Be Explicit or Fail
description: **CanonRS CLI must never rely on silent fallback paths.**

title: CSS Bundle Size Drift Must Be Monitored
description: canonrs.bundle.css MUST have a declared size expectation.

title: No Runtime CSS Regeneration
description: CanonRS CSS MUST be generated at build time only.

title: Workspace Crate Boundaries Must Be Explicitly Declared
description: Every crate inside the CanonRS workspace MUST declare its architectural role explicitly in its `Cargo.toml`.

title: Behavior Registry Is the Single Runtime Entry Point
description: **All behaviors MUST be registered exclusively through the Behavior Registry and initialized via a single runtime bootstrap call.**

title: Interactive Owns All State — Behavior Owns None
description: **All feature state MUST live inside Interactive. Behavior MUST NOT maintain internal mutable state beyond ephemeral runtime tracking.**

title: Primitives Must Never Generate IDs
description: **Primitives MUST receive IDs externally and MUST NOT generate, mutate, or auto-derive IDs internally.**

title: Data Attributes Are Contract, Not Styling Mechanism
description: **Data-attributes MUST represent structural or behavioral contracts, not visual styling shortcuts.**

title: Behavior Cleanup Is Mandatory and Deterministic
description: **Every Behavior must implement deterministic cleanup of listeners, guards, and side-effects.**

title: Custom Events Are Public Runtime Contracts
description: **All canon:* custom events are part of the public runtime API and semver-bound.**

title: Behavior Must Never Traverse Beyond Its Container Scope
description: **Behavior must operate strictly within its container element boundary.**

title: Behavior Execution Must Be Idempotent
description: **Running register() multiple times must not change system state.**

title: Global Mutable State Is Forbidden in wasm Scope
description: **No global mutable state may exist in wasm runtime.**

title: MutationObserver Requires Explicit Architectural Justification
description: **MutationObserver must never be used implicitly.**

title: Interactive Features Must Be Strictly Isolated
description: **Each feature must operate independently without signal cross-coupling.**

title: Interactive Hooks Must Be Container-Bound
description: **Hooks must bind strictly to container_id.**

title: Interactive Must Remain Deterministic Under SSR Without wasm
description: **Interactive rendering must not depend on wasm execution.**

title: Interactive Must Not Emit DOM Events
description: **Interactive layer must never dispatch DOM events directly.**

title: Primitive Must Not Encode Design Tokens in Rust
description: **Primitives must never hardcode design tokens, CSS variables, colors, spacing, or layout values in Rust.**

title: UI Layer Must Not Be Aware of Behavior Layer
description: **UI must never import, reference, register, or conditionally depend on Behavior modules.**

title: Interactive Must Be Safe Under Feature Flag Removal
description: **Disabling any optional feature must not alter structural rendering or break hydration.**

title: Cross-Crate Layer Leakage Is Forbidden
description: **A crate must never access another crate’s internal layer (primitive/ui/interactive/behavior) bypassing public boundaries.**
